title: Liver Tissue Tissue Microarray (TMA) Embedding and Sectioning --University of Minnesota TMCs

materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6hjuv","text":"FFPE tissue blocks with sufficient tissue (width and depth) for TMA construction","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5l7qo","text":"Beecher Manual Tissue Arrayer (MTA-1)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7fhvb","text":"Biopsy punch (2 mm)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The smallest capture dimension of all the spatial platforms for the multiplatform experiment was 6.5x 6.5 mm. It was determined that four x 2 mm tissue cores with 2.8 mm spacing could successfully fit within these dimensions.
H&E of each tissue block for the TMA was examined by pathologist and 2 mm area determined and annotated on the FFPE block.
2 mm cores removed from FFPE block using biopsy punch
The Beecher Manual Tissue Arrayer (MTA-1) was used for TMA construction.
Four tissue cores were embedded in a paraffin block in a column/ grid format.
To aid in orientation, a 0.6mm core of pig tongue tissue was included as a marker.
creator: Allie Abdellatif
